name: Pookie's Liver and Onions
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Beef liver, cut into one thin slice
2 tbsp Cooking oil (preferably peanut, corn, or sunflower)
recipeInstructions:
Even if you dislike liver and onions, this tender, soulful version will surprise you. If you enjoy liver and onions, get ready for a starburst effect. This recipe is inspired by Pookie Turner's dish.
1. Season the liver with salt and pepper to taste. Dredge lightly in flour.
2. Heat oil in a heavy skillet over high heat until fragrant (about 1 minute). Add liver and fry until lightly browned on both sides for about 1 minute per side. Remove liver and set aside.
3. Clean the skillet and return it to the stove over medium heat until dry. Add butter and melt it. Add green onions, garlic, and sliced onions; stir-fry until the onions are translucent (about 6 minutes).
4. Sprinkle flour over the mixture and cook briefly until the flour turns golden and loses its raw aroma (about 1 minute). Do not let the flour brown. Stir in beef broth, Kitchen Bouquet, salt, and pepper. Turn heat to medium-high and bring to a boil. Cook, stirring, until the liquid thickens slightly (about 6 minutes). Adjust seasoning if necessary.
5. Return the liver to the pan and cook on both sides for 30 seconds each (or 90 seconds per side if the liver is thick). Remove from heat and serve immediately with boiled rice.
Serves 2.
From "Regional American Classics", California Culinary Academy, Chevron Chemical Company, 1987. Posted by Stephen Ceideberg; November 3 1992.
